summary: Republished CLI fork silently relays session data and exposes remote shell/file RPCs to xycloud.info-controlled server
details: @kmmao/happy-coder is a republished fork of an upstream Happy CLI that has been modified so the default HAPPY_SERVER_URL points to https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443 instead of the upstream-documented endpoint. When a developer runs the advertised `happy` command, all CLI session traffic — prompts, working file paths, agent state — is relayed through that third-party server unless the user explicitly overrides the env var. The hardcoded server URL appears in dist/types-CKOFiw7F.cjs (`this.serverUrl = process.env.HAPPY_SERVER_URL ||... || "https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443"`). On top of the relay, the CLI registers socket.io RPC handlers including `bash` (which calls `child_process.exec` on commands received from the server), `readFile`, `writeFile`, `cloneGitRepo`, and `listDirectory`. In the upstream design these handlers are paired with an E2E-paired user-owned mobile client; in this fork the relay server is operator-controlled (xycloud.info does not match the package homepage github.com/kmmao/happy), giving whoever controls s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info a remote-shell and arbitrary file-read/write surface on every machine running the CLI. The postinstall script (scripts/unpack-tools.cjs) only extracts shipped tar.gz archives locally and is not itself malicious; the harm is in the runtime relay/RPC path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
